title: Non-uniqueness of Leray solutions of the forced Navier-Stokes equations
speaker: Dallas Albritton

proof_stages.label: Leray-Hopf setting and uniqueness problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up forced incompressible Navier-Stokes, Leray-Hopf and suitable weak solutions with energy inequalities, and frames uniqueness of Leray continuations as a central problem connected to possible blowup.

proof_stages.label: Self-similar unstable-manifold mechanism
proof_stages.summary: Nonuniqueness is interpreted as extreme instability: an unstable manifold in logarithmic self-similar time has trajectories emerging from a steady profile at tau=-infinity, which becomes physical time zero after undoing the similarity variables.

proof_stages.label: Forced self-similar theorem formulation
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is sharpened to the construction of a smooth compactly supported divergence-free similarity profile whose linearization has an unstable eigenvalue, with the common force obtained by plugging the chosen background into the equation.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to unstable forced Euler steady states
proof_stages.summary: A large-amplitude parameter makes the Euler nonlinear terms dominate viscosity and similarity-drift terms, so the main spectral task reduces to producing an unstable steady state for three-dimensional forced Euler and then perturbing back to Navier-Stokes in similarity variables.

proof_stages.label: Unstable two-dimensional vortex building block
proof_stages.summary: Using Vishik's two-dimensional vortex instability, explained through the Rayleigh stability equation and neutral-mode bifurcation mechanism, one obtains a smooth compactly supported Euler vortex with an unstable eigenvalue.

proof_stages.label: Large-radius lift to a three-dimensional vortex ring
proof_stages.summary: The two-dimensional vortex is placed far from the symmetry axis as an axisymmetric vortex ring, exploiting that axisymmetric Euler without swirl formally converges to two-dimensional Euler as the radius tends to infinity, with small corrections for divergence and stretching.

proof_stages.label: Spectral transfer by resolvent convergence
proof_stages.summary: The linearized vortex-ring operator is compared with the two-dimensional linearized Euler operator on weighted spaces, and convergence of resolvents implies convergence of spectral projections and hence persistence of an unstable eigenvalue for large ring radius.

proof_stages.label: Nonlinear unstable trajectory and Leray pair
proof_stages.summary: After transferring instability to the velocity formulation, a fixed-point unstable-manifold ansatz constructs a nontrivial nonlinear trajectory whose correction is higher order than the unstable linear mode, and transforming back yields two suitable Leray-Hopf solutions with the same force and zero initial data.
